characters.name: Abby
characters.description: Abbyis a recurring character in thesecond seasonofSports Night. She is played by guest starJayne Brookand debuts in "Kafelnikov". She isDan Rydell's psychiatrist. She is a psychiatrist in New York. She meetsDan Rydellin a bar and gives him her card. He initially assumes that she wanted a date and then realizes that she was suggesting that he needed therapy.[1]

She is a studio technician forSports Nighton CSC. She takes part inDan Rydell's passover celebration, playing "Rose".[1] * - Uncredited appearance

characters.name: Billie Tasker
characters.description: Billie Taskeris a minor character in thesecond seasonofSports Night. She is played by guest starBari Hochwaldand only appears in "Cliff Gardner". She is a television executive at CSC. She is present for meetings with theSports Nightstaff where the network is suggesting cutbacks. She,J.J.andRay MitchelapproachSam Donovanas a candidate to take over fromIsaac Jaffeas managing editor but Sam rejects the proposal.[1]

characters.name: Monica Brazelton
characters.description: Monica Brazeltonis a minor character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. She is played by guest starJanel Moloneyand only appears in "The Six Southern Gentlemen of Tennessee". She is an Assistant Wardrobe Supervisor forSports Night. Monica takes anchorCasey McCallto task after he appears on the talk showThe Viewand fails to credit the wardrobe department for choosing his ties. She reminds him that many other people work on the show with little recognition. He responds by thanking the crew during a Christmas episode.[1]

characters.name: Burke
characters.description: Burkeis a minor character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. He is played by guest starJon Simmonsand only appears in "Mary Pat Shelby". He is a sports agent and represents football playerChristian Patrick, who is infamous for sexually assaulting a woman named Mary Pat Shelby. Burke and his partnerEvansarrange for their clientChristian Patrickto be interviewed onSports Night. They stipulate that he cannot be asked about the sexual assault at the last minute after the show has been promoted.Natalie Hurleyvisits him at his home stadium to conduct a pre-interview. When he thinks they are alone in the locker room he makes sexual advances and grabs her wrist. The incident was witnessed and reported. Executive ProducerDana Whitakernegotiates with Burke and Evans to conceal the incident in exchange for a more probing interview. After some debate theSports Nightteam decline to interview him despite the additional concessions. Natalie eventually comes forward and presses charges against him.[1]

characters.name: Catherine
characters.description: Catherineis a minor character in thesecond seasonofSports Night. She is played by guest starAlanna Ubachand only appears in "Bells and a Siren." She is a publicist and handlesDan Rydellwhen he  hires her firm. She convinces Dan to attend a restaurant opening in New Jersey. He is disappointed with the event and decides to fire her. She honestly appraises his distaste for her industry and his hypocrisy, convincing him to retain her services.[1]

characters.name: Celia
characters.description: Celiais a recurring character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. She is played by co-starJosephine Barnsdaleand debuts in "Dana and the Deep Blue Sea". She is an analyst atCSCand a colleague ofRebecca Wells. She greetsDan Rydellwhen he visits Rebecca; Rebecca is frustrated that all of her colleagues are friendly with Dan.[1] She says hey to Dan again when he returns after the bomb scare.[2]

characters.name: Claire
characters.description: Claireis a minor character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. She is played by co-starNina Jane Barryand only appears in "Pilot". She is a production assistant forSports Night. She is seen working in the background during the pilot episode. She delivers rundown sheets to the crew and takes a phone message for Casey.[1]

characters.name: Corbin
characters.description: Corbinis a minor character in thesecond seasonofSports Night. He is played by guest starCharlie Finnand only appears in "Special Powers". He is an Intern forSports Night. His cousinsJ.J.works for theCSCnetwork and gets him an intern position atSports Night. He performs poorly andNatalie HurleyordersJeremy Goodwinto fire him. Jeremy decides to give him another chance. Corbin's disinterested attitude is unchanged and Jeremy does eventually fire him when he insults Natalie.[1]

characters.name: Evans
characters.description: Evansis a minor character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. He is played by guest starRay Wiseand only appears in "Mary Pat Shelby". He is a sports agent and represents football playerChristian Patrick, who is infamous for sexually assaulting a woman named Mary Pat Shelby. Evans and his partnerBurkearrange for their clientChristian Patrickto be interviewed onSports Night. They stipulate that he cannot be asked about the sexual assault at the last minute after the show has been promoted.Natalie Hurleyvisits him at his home stadium to conduct a pre-interview. When he thinks they are alone in the locker room he makes sexual advances and grabs her wrist. The incident was witnessed and reported. Executive ProducerDana Whitakernegotiates with Burke and Evans to conceal the incident in exchange for a more probing interview. After some debate theSports Nightteam decline to interview him despite the additional concessions. Natalie eventually comes forward and presses charges against him.[1]

characters.name: Jeremy Goodwin
characters.description: Jeremy Goodwinis a major character in thefirstandsecondseasons ofSports Night. He is played by starring cast memberJoshua Malinaand debuts in "Pilot". He is a live television producer. He is an Associate Producer forSports Night. Jeremy is from Boston. He attended Amherst and received a degree in applied mathematics. He dates Natalie, with multiple breakups, for a large portion of the series. He is Jewish; his family includes his sister Louise, who is deaf, and their parents, who divorce due to Jeremy's father revealing a long-running affair with another woman. ROLE.[1]

characters.name: Homeless man
characters.description: AHomeless manis an unnamed minor character in thefirst seasonofSports Night. He is played by guest starFelton Perryand only appears in "The Quality of Mercy at 29K". He sneaks into theSports Nightoffices when security staff clear the homeless out of the CSC building lobby.Sports NightanchorDan Rydellfinds him hiding in his office. He is initially startled but then offers him a turkey sandwich. Dan is afraid when he pulls a switchblade but he just uses it to share the sandwich. They watch climbers reaching the summit of Mount Everest together.[1]

characters.name: Howard
characters.description: Howardis a minor character in thesecond seasonofSports Night. he is played by co-starTom Cavanaghand only appears in "The Giants Win the Pennant, the Giants Win the Pennant!". He works atCSCand datesPixley Robinson. He works atCSCand is an acquaintance ofNatalie Hurley. He has a date withPixley Robinsonwhich is marred when she reveals that she recently datedCasey McCallbecause Howard is a huge fan ofSports Night. When Casey arrives by chance he is unable to containment his excitement and Pixley sends him to get their coats.[1]
